title: A Kernel-Clean Lean Mechanization of Classical Lottery in Action and the Wakker-Debreu-Koopmans Representation Layer
abstract: This paper presents a Lean 4 and Mathlib formalization of additive representation theory behind Classical Lottery in Action and the Wakker-Debreu-Koopmans layer. It gives a machine-checked boundary between ordinal axioms of additive conjoint measurement and the additional structural input needed for representation results.

title: The Winner's Bliss in Common-Value Auctions under Horizontal Differentiation
abstract: The authors study common-value auctions with horizontally differentiated bidder preferences. In a two-bidder setting, winning can convey good news about the object's value to the winner, producing a winner's bliss that differs from the standard winner's curse logic.

title: Strategic Type Spaces
abstract: This paper develops a strategic foundation for information in incomplete-information games. It defines strategic quotients that are sufficient for best responses and proves existence and essential uniqueness of a minimal Strategic Type Space with a recursive finite-automaton structure.

title: Learn to Match: Two-Sided Matching with Temporally Extended Feedback
abstract: This paper models two-sided matching as a partially observable Markov game with interviews, repeated interaction, latent profiles, and endogenous continuation or dissolution. The Learn2Match benchmark evaluates policies using regret, social welfare, and information-friction loss.

title: Fairness and Strategy-Proofness in Automated Market Makers
abstract: The paper shows that no aggregation rule for weighted-product automated market makers with at least three assets can be both fair and strategy-proof. The result connects Arrovian fairness, provider preferences over pools, and impossibility results for externally Bayesian pooling.

title: Multi-Dimensional Matching in Market Design
abstract: This paper proposes a computational mechanism for multi-dimensional matching markets where agents report preferences over object features. It uses singular value decomposition to reduce feature-based matching to an effectively one-dimensional problem and studies welfare, truthfulness, symmetry, and robustness.

title: Contracting with Imperfect Commitment: Minimal Canonical Contracts
abstract: The authors ask when imperfect commitment is equivalent to full commitment in contract design. They study contracts with committed baselines and bounded discretionary adjustments, characterize optimal single-principal contracts, and develop an extended taxation principle for tractable analysis.

title: Supply Chain Coordination Mechanism Design: Consensus Planning Protocol Meets Vickrey-Clarke-Groves Mechanism
abstract: This paper combines Vickrey-Clarke-Groves mechanisms with the Consensus Planning Protocol to support truthful and efficient collaboration between a retailer and vendors. It also introduces an activity-fee design to improve revenue properties while preserving incentive and efficiency features.
